an4.36:1.1: I have heard that on one occasion the Blessed One was traveling along the road between Ukkaṭṭha and Setabya,
an4.36:1.2: and Doṇa the brahman was also traveling along the road between Ukkaṭṭha and Setabya.
an4.36:1.3: Doṇa the brahman saw, in the Blessed One’s footprints, wheels with 1,000 spokes, together with rims and hubs, complete in all their features.
an4.36:1.4: On seeing them, the thought occurred to him,
an4.36:1.6: These are not the footprints of a human being!”
an4.36:1.7: Then the Blessed One, leaving the road, went to sit at the root of a certain tree—his legs crossed, his body erect, with mindfulness established to the fore.
an4.36:1.8: Then Doṇa, following the Blessed One’s footprints, saw him sitting at the root of the tree: confident, inspiring confidence, his senses calmed, his mind calmed, having attained the utmost control & tranquility, tamed, guarded, his senses restrained, a nāga.
an4.36:1.9: On seeing him, he went to him and said,
an4.36:2.2: “No, brahman, I am not a deva.”
an4.36:2.8: “No, brahman, I am not a human being.”
an4.36:3.2: you answer, ‘No, brahman, I am not a deva.’
an4.36:3.4: you answer, ‘No, brahman, I am not a gandhabba.’
an4.36:3.6: you answer, ‘No, brahman, I am not a yakkha.’
an4.36:3.7: When asked, ‘Are you a human being?’
an4.36:3.8: you answer, ‘No, brahman, I am not a human being.’
an4.36:3.9: Then what sort of being are you?”
an4.36:4.1: “Brahman, the effluents by which—if they were not abandoned—I would be a deva: Those are abandoned by me, their root destroyed, made like a palmyra stump, deprived of the conditions of development, not destined for future arising.
an4.36:4.2: The effluents by which—if they were not abandoned—I would be a gandhabba… a yakkha… a human being: Those are abandoned by me, their root destroyed, made like a palmyra stump, deprived of the conditions of development, not destined for future arising.
an4.36:4.3: Just like a red, blue, or white lotus—born in the water, grown in the water, rising up above the water—stands unsmeared by the water,
an4.36:4.4: in the same way I—born in the world, grown in the world, having overcome the world—live unsmeared by the world.
an4.36:5.1: “The effluents by which I would go
an4.36:5.2: to a deva-state, or become a gandhabba in the sky,
an4.36:5.5: Those have been destroyed by me,
an4.36:6.1: Like a blue lotus, rising up,
an4.36:6.3: unsmeared am I by the world,
